passes.description: Subquery: build per-orderkey quantity sums, emit qualifying orderkey set
Main query: scan lineitem, filter to qualifying set, probe indexes, group-aggregate

filter.notes: ~300 qualifying orderkeys out of 15M unique; check is O(1) hash lookup, negligible cost

joins.notes: Semi-join filter against tiny qualifying set; effectively a hash_set membership check per lineitem row
Only ~few thousand lookups for qualifying rows; fetch o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, orders_row
Only ~300 unique customer lookups; fetch c_name, cust_row

aggregation.notes: Group key is effectively l_orderkey since each order has exactly one (c_name, c_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice); resolve other GROUP BY columns from stored orders_row/cust_row after merge

join_order.notes: Bloom filter not needed; set is tiny and fits in L1 cache
Pre-built open-addressing hash index; ~300-500 total probes; mmap the 256MB index file
Pre-built open-addressing hash index; ~300 unique probes; mmap the 32MB index file

filters.notes: Extremely selective; eliminates ~99.9995% of lineitem rows after pass 1

data_structures.pass1_qty_sum_map: compact_hash_map(pre_sized:16777216) — thread-local per worker, keyed int32_t → double
data_structures.qualifying_set: hash_set<int32_t>(capacity:1024) — built after pass 1 merge; fits entirely in L1/L2 cache
data_structures.pass2_group_map: compact_hash_map(pre_sized:1024) — thread-local per worker, keyed int32_t → {sum_qty:double, orders_row:int32, cust_row:int32}

parallelism.notes: Each thread owns its row range for both passes; thread-local maps avoid contention; merge is cheap given tiny qualifying set

indexes.access: mmap, random probes only for qualifying rows (~few thousand total)
mmap, random probes only for qualifying orders (~300 total)

column_reads.notes: orders and customer columns read only for qualifying rows (~300-500 rows); negligible I/O

output.sort_strategy: std::sort on ~300-500 result structs — trivially fast
output.date_decode: convert o_orderdate (int32 days since 1970-01-01) to YYYY-MM-DD for output only (100 rows)
